name: MS-QRD-003: DNS Tunneling and Exfiltration via DNS — Covert Channel
description: Detects potential DNS tunneling activity by identifying hosts generating an abnormally high volume of DNS queries within a short time window, querying an excessive number of unique subdomains under a single parent domain, or generating DNS queries with unusually long subdomain labels. Attackers use DNS tunneling tools such as iodine, dnscat2, and Cobalt Strike DNS beacons to exfiltrate data or establish C2 channels through DNS, bypassing traditional network controls.
author: MilliSec Blue Team — Rihad Ebilov

mitre_attack.subtechnique: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Exfiltration Over Symmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol

qradar.aql: SELECT sourceip AS "Source IP", destinationip AS "DNS Server IP", LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS "Log Source", COUNT(*) AS "Total DNS Queries", COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) AS "Unique DNS Servers", AVG(LENGTH(QIDNAME(qid))) AS "Avg Query Name Length", MIN(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "First Seen", MAX(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "Last Seen" FROM events WHERE (CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%dns%' OR destinationport = 53 OR destinationport = 5353) AND sourceip IS NOT NULL AND sourceip != '0.0.0.0' AND (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', sourceip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', sourceip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', sourceip)) GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) HAVING COUNT(*) >= 200 OR COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) >= 5 ORDER BY "Total DNS Queries" DESC LAST 10 MINUTES
qradar.offense_relevance: Abnormally high DNS query volume or use of multiple DNS servers from a single internal host is a strong indicator of DNS tunneling. Tools like dnscat2 and iodine encode data in DNS query labels, often generating hundreds of queries per minute. Correlate with network flow data to verify outbound DNS traffic volume exceeds normal baseline.
response_actions: Capture and inspect DNS query content using Wireshark or network tap
Block outbound DNS to all servers except authorized resolvers at firewall
Isolate the source host for forensic analysis
Search for dnscat2, iodine, or similar DNS tunneling binaries on the host
Check DNS server logs for encoded subdomains (long, random-looking labels)
qradar.false_positive_notes: CDN and cloud services with high DNS query rates (Microsoft 365, Akamai). Software update services polling frequently. Misconfigured DNS clients with short TTL values causing cache misses. Browser pre-fetching DNS for visited sites. Adjust threshold upward (500+) if baseline is naturally high.
qradar.tuning_notes: Primary indicator: COUNT(*) >= 200 queries in 10 minutes to same DNS server. Secondary: COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) >= 5 unique DNS servers (bypassing local resolver). For higher precision, integrate with DNS log sources that provide the actual query name field and add: AND LENGTH(url) > 50 to detect long subdomain labels used for data encoding.
